name: T-18

description: MS-1 (T-18) was the first mass-produced Soviet tank, created on the basis of the French Renault FT. It had a classic layout with a turret capable of rotating 360°. The vehicle was used in combat operations in the Far East and in border conflicts, but by the late 1930s it had become obsolete. Despite weak armor and low speed, the MS-1 became an important stage in the development of Soviet tank building.

name: T-26
armor:
Hull armor (front / side / rear) - 15 / 15 / 6 mm.
Turret armor (front / side / rear) - 15 / 15 / 15 mm.

guns_and_spead:
45 mm 20K gun | 12 rounds/min.
 7.62 mm DT machine gun (coaxial) | 600 rounds/min.
description: The T-26 was a Soviet light tank of the 1930s, developed on the basis of the British Vickers Mk.E. It had powerful armament for its class, but weak armor. It was used in combat from the early 1930s until the beginning of the Great Patriotic War, where its effectiveness decreased due to its outdated design and weak protection.

name: T-34
armor:
Hull armor (front / side / rear) - 45 / 45 / 40 mm.
Turret armor (front / side / rear) - 45 / 45 / 45 mm.

76 mm L-11 gun | 7 rounds/min.
 7.62 mm DT machine gun (coaxial) | 600 rounds/min.
description: The T-34 was a medium tank that became the backbone of Soviet armored forces during World War II. It had a powerful gun, sloped armor, and high maneuverability. The 1940 version had the L-11 gun, but it was later replaced by the more powerful F-34. The tank proved effective against Wehrmacht armored vehicles and played a key role in the victory of the USSR.

name: T-55
armor:
Hull armor (front / side / rear) - 100 / 80 / 45 mm.
Turret armor (front / side / rear) - 200 / 160 / 66 mm.

100 mm D-10T2S gun | 6 rounds/min.

description: The T-55 is a main battle tank created on the basis of the T-54 main and medium tank. It was adopted by the Soviet Army of the USSR Armed Forces in 1958. It was produced from 1958 to 1979. The T-55 was the world's first mass-produced tank equipped with an automatic nuclear protection system — a pioneer of a new generation of combat vehicles capable of operating under conditions of enemy nuclear weapon use.

name: T-62
armor:
Hull armor (front / side / rear) - 100 / 80 / 45 mm.

description: The T-62 is the first variant of the T-62 family of main battle tanks. As a further development of the T-55 family of main battle tanks, the first T-62 variant retained many structural elements of its predecessor, including a low profile and thick turret armor.

Thanks to its new turret and Kontakt-5 explosive reactive armor, it visually differs from its T-64 predecessors and boasts strong protection against both chemical and kinetic ammunition.

Due to its high weight and weak suspension, the tank had poor mobility on rough terrain. Only 20 units were produced, as Germany lagged behind in the development of armored vehicles.

The M48A2 modification was fitted with an AVI-1790-8 gasoline engine with a power output of 825 hp, and the fuel tank capacity was increased to 1,440 liters, which increased the cruising range to 400 km. The tank also received an Allison CD-850-5 transmission. Another distinctive feature was the installation of three return rollers instead of five.

The history of this tank began in the second half of the 1950s, when the leadership of several European countries, including France, West Germany, and Italy, which had a strong production base, decided to create a medium tank according to common European standards. These standards implied light anti-shell armor for the future vehicle, as well as high mobility.

This version was developed by Krauss-Maffei Wegmann, which launched it into production in 1985. Unlike previous modifications, the 2A4 received a new turret with enhanced armor protection, a digital fire-control system, and many other minor changes that improved the vehicle's ease of operation.

description: The Stormer HVM is a Starstreak anti-aircraft missile system mounted on the chassis of the British FV4333 armored personnel carrier. In the early 1980s, the British Army had a need for a SAM system. The designers installed launchers for the Starstreak system on the already proven FV4333 armored personnel carrier chassis. Tests did not reveal any serious shortcomings in the vehicle, and in 1997 it was adopted into service.
